file_name: BOLIVARMININGCORP_05_23_2003-EX-2.1-VISP WEB SITE BUILDING AND HOSTING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement. However, KCI may terminate the agreement at any time with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the termination clause. The original text specifies a renewal process with a 90-day notice for non-renewal. The added sentence allows KCI to terminate the agreement at any time with only a 30-day notice, contradicting the implied commitment to the full term and the renewal process. This creates uncertainty about the actual termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 TERM This agreement shall be for a term of five years from the date of this document. This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement. However, KCI may terminate the agreement at any time with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows KCI to terminate the agreement at any time with 30 days' notice, contradicting the original term of five years and the 90-day notice requirement for non-renewal. This creates a direct conflict regarding the termination conditions, as the contradicted text originally stated a five-year term with a specific renewal process, which is now undermined by the new termination clause. This falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since the different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.changed_text: KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge. However, Provider reserves the right to charge KCI's customers for premium technical support services after the first year of service.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that KCI's customers receive services 'for no charge.' The added sentence allows Provider to charge KCI's customers for premium support after the first year. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the costs associated with the services, potentially leading to disputes about billing and the scope of the initial agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3 PROVIDER'S RESPONSIBILITIES Provider agrees to provide KCI with VISP Private labeled website building and maintenance capabilities. Deliverables shall include: Sales collateral materials including brochures and flyers to help KCI sign customers to the service. A fully staffed back end including; Billing, real time credit card transactions, administrative reports, Customer and technical support, Online Customer Account Maintenance, Private labeled E-Mail Server, Administrative E-Mail Addresses, vacation e-mail, Web based and POP Access, Online Interfaces, a private labeled Internet portal for KCI and include an affiliate program. KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge. However, Provider reserves the right to charge KCI's customers for premium technical support services after the first year of service.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that KCI's customers receive services 'for no charge'. The changed text adds a sentence allowing Provider to charge KCI's customers for premium support after the first year. This directly contradicts the 'for no charge' statement, creating a clear inconsistency in pricing and service terms. The contradicted text includes the changed text, thus highlighting the contradiction within that same section.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business. However, KCI cannot assign this agreement to any company that is in direct competition with Provider without prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows assignment to affiliated entities without consent. The added sentence restricts KCI's ability to assign the agreement to competitors of Provider, adding a condition not present initially. This creates a contradiction because the 'notwithstanding the foregoing' clause implies unconditional assignment to affiliated entities, yet the added clause introduces a specific restriction that could apply to an affiliated entity. This creates uncertainty about the conditions under which assignment is permissible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8 ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns; provided, however, that no rights or obligations hereunder, including but not limited to Licensee's Subscriber accounts, shall be assigned or transferred, in whole or in part, by either of the parties hereto to any person, firm or corporation without prior written consent by the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business. However, KCI cannot assign this agreement to any company that is in direct competition with Provider without prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition on assignment that directly contradicts the 'notwithstanding the foregoing' clause, creating an inconsistency. The original text allows assignment to affiliated entities without consent, implying an absolute right. The added sentence restricts KCI's ability to assign the agreement to competitors of Provider, adding a condition not present initially. This creates a contradiction because the 'notwithstanding the foregoing' clause implies unconditional assignment to affiliated entities, yet the added clause introduces a specific restriction that could apply to an affiliated entity. This contradiction falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as defined, since the modified section now conflicts directly with another section of the contract regarding assignment conditions, particularly the 'notwithstanding the foregoing' which appears at the start of the contradicted text.
